edges.description: Mitogenic estrogen signaling favors endometrial hyperplasia when not balanced by progesterone
Hyperplastic endometrium provides the proliferative substrate in which driver alterations can support malignant outgrowth
Recurrent driver mutations sustain malignant growth and survival
WNT transcriptional activation contributes to malignant epithelial growth
Ultramutated POLE-driven tumors constitute one molecular route to endometrial carcinoma
Checkpoint-mediated immune suppression allows immunogenic endometrial carcinoma cells to persist and progress
Copy-number high TP53-mutant tumors form an aggressive serous-like route to endometrial carcinoma
edges.source: TP53 Mutation and Copy-Number High State

edges.description: Sustained malignant epithelial proliferation produces the defining carcinoma phenotype

edges.target: TP53 Mutation and Copy-Number High State

nodes.description: Immunogenic endometrial carcinomas, especially the mismatch repair-deficient/MSI-hypermutated subtype represented in the dedicated MSI-High Endometrial Cancer entry, can co-opt PD-1/PD-L1 checkpoint signaling to suppress anti-tumor T cell activity. This parent-level node provides the mechanism targeted by PD-1 blockade regimens without duplicating the full subtype-specific MSI-H mechanism chain.

biological_processes: Negative Regulation of T Cell Mediated Immunity

evidence.reference_title: Dostarlimab for Primary Advanced or Recurrent Endometrial Cancer.
evidence.snippet: Dostarlimab plus carboplatin-paclitaxel significantly increased progression-free survival among patients with primary advanced or recurrent endometrial cancer, with a substantial benefit in the dMMR-MSI-H population.

evidence.reference_title: Integrated genomic characterization of endometrial carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Most endometrioid tumours had few copy number alterations or TP53 mutations, but frequent mutations in PTEN, CTNNB1, PIK3CA, ARID1A and KRAS and novel mutations in the SWI/SNF chromatin remodelling complex gene ARID5B.

nodes.description: Activating CTNNB1 alterations are recurrent in endometrioid/copy-number-low endometrial carcinoma and drive beta-catenin-dependent WNT transcriptional programs that support malignant growth.

evidence.reference_title: Integrated genomic characterization of endometrial carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Most endometrioid tumours had few copy number alterations or TP53 mutations, but frequent mutations in PTEN, CTNNB1, PIK3CA, ARID1A and KRAS and novel mutations in the SWI/SNF chromatin remodelling complex gene ARID5B.

nodes.description: Dostarlimab added to carboplatin-paclitaxel improves progression-free survival in primary advanced or recurrent endometrial cancer, with the largest benefit in the dMMR/MSI-H population.

evidence.reference_title: Dostarlimab for Primary Advanced or Recurrent Endometrial Cancer.
evidence.snippet: Dostarlimab plus carboplatin-paclitaxel significantly increased progression-free survival among patients with primary advanced or recurrent endometrial cancer, with a substantial benefit in the dMMR-MSI-H population.

nodes.description: Primary epithelial malignancy arising from the endometrium.

evidence.snippet: Endometrial cancer is the most common gynaecological tumour in developed countries, and its incidence is increasing.

nodes.description: Prolonged progesterone deficiency and estrogen-driven proliferation can produce endometrial hyperplasia, including atypical hyperplasia/endometrial intraepithelial neoplasia, which can progress to endometrioid carcinoma.

evidence.reference_title: Understanding obesity and endometrial cancer risk: opportunities for prevention.
evidence.snippet: Conditions that are accompanied by prolonged progesterone deficiencies therefore promote endometrial proliferation and increase the risk of endometrial hyperplasia and its progression to endometrial cancer

nodes.description: Combination systemic therapy for advanced endometrial carcinoma after prior platinum-based chemotherapy, with survival benefit over physician's-choice chemotherapy.

evidence.reference_title: Lenvatinib plus Pembrolizumab for Advanced Endometrial Cancer.
evidence.snippet: Lenvatinib plus pembrolizumab led to significantly longer progression-free survival and overall survival than chemotherapy among patients with advanced endometrial cancer.

evidence.reference_title: Integrated genomic characterization of endometrial carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Most endometrioid tumours had few copy number alterations or TP53 mutations, but frequent mutations in PTEN, CTNNB1, PIK3CA, ARID1A and KRAS and novel mutations in the SWI/SNF chromatin remodelling complex gene ARID5B.

evidence.reference_title: Integrated genomic characterization of endometrial carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: A subset of endometrioid tumours that we identified had a markedly increased transversion mutation frequency and newly identified hotspot mutations in POLE.

nodes.description: Somatic POLE exonuclease-domain mutations impair polymerase proofreading during DNA replication, creating an ultramutated molecular class with very high mutation burden and generally favorable prognosis.

evidence.reference_title: Integrated genomic characterization of endometrial carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: A subset of endometrioid tumours that we identified had a markedly increased transversion mutation frequency and newly identified hotspot mutations in POLE. Our results classified endometrial cancers into four categories: POLE ultramutated, microsatellite instability hypermutated, copy-number low, and copy-number high.

evidence.reference_title: Integrated genomic characterization of endometrial carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Most endometrioid tumours had few copy number alterations or TP53 mutations, but frequent mutations in PTEN, CTNNB1, PIK3CA, ARID1A and KRAS and novel mutations in the SWI/SNF chromatin remodelling complex gene ARID5B.

nodes.description: Endometrioid tumors are enriched for PTEN, PIK3CA, KRAS, and ARID1A alterations. Loss of PTEN restraint and cooperating PI3K-pathway lesions promote survival, growth, and progression of malignant endometrial epithelial cells.

evidence.reference_title: Integrated genomic characterization of endometrial carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Most endometrioid tumours had few copy number alterations or TP53 mutations, but frequent mutations in PTEN, CTNNB1, PIK3CA, ARID1A and KRAS

nodes.description: Pembrolizumab added to paclitaxel-carboplatin improves progression-free survival for advanced or recurrent endometrial carcinoma, with benefit in both dMMR and pMMR cohorts.

evidence.reference_title: Pembrolizumab plus Chemotherapy in Advanced Endometrial Cancer.
evidence.snippet: In patients with advanced or recurrent endometrial cancer, the addition of pembrolizumab to standard chemotherapy resulted in significantly longer progression-free survival than with chemotherapy alone.

evidence.reference_title: Integrated genomic characterization of endometrial carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Uterine serous tumours and ∼25% of high-grade endometrioid tumours had extensive copy number alterations, few DNA methylation changes, low oestrogen receptor/progesterone receptor levels, and frequent TP53 mutations.

nodes.description: Serous and serous-like endometrial carcinomas are characterized by TP53 mutation, extensive copy-number alterations, low ER and PR expression, and aggressive biology. This molecular state underlies poorer prognosis and distinct therapeutic risk stratification.
nodes.id: TP53 Mutation and Copy-Number High State

evidence.reference_title: Integrated genomic characterization of endometrial carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Uterine serous tumours and ∼25% of high-grade endometrioid tumours had extensive copy number alterations, few DNA methylation changes, low oestrogen receptor/progesterone receptor levels, and frequent TP53 mutations.

nodes.description: Estrogen-driven precursor proliferation and recurrent oncogenic driver alterations converge on sustained proliferation and survival of malignant endometrial epithelial cells.

biological_processes: positive regulation of cell population proliferation

evidence.reference_title: Protein Kinase C α Modulates Estrogen-Receptor-Dependent Transcription and Proliferation in Endometrial Cancer Cells.
evidence.snippet: knockdown of PKC α reduced ER-dependent gene expression and inhibited estrogen-induced proliferation of endometrial cancer cells.

nodes.description: Hyperestrogenic states expose endometrial epithelium to mitogenic estrogen signaling without sufficient progesterone counter-regulation, promoting sustained glandular proliferation.

evidence.reference_title: Obesity, endogenous hormones, and endometrial cancer risk: a synthetic review.
evidence.snippet: These relationships can all be interpreted in the light of the "unopposed estrogen" hypothesis, which proposes that endometrial cancer may develop as a result of the mitogenic effects of estrogens, when these are insufficiently counterbalanced by progesterone.
